items.title: Corewell, UnitedHealthcare end contract dispute that put hospitals out of network for months - Crain's Detroit
items.reason: This item directly addresses a payer-provider contract dispute involving network status and hospital reimbursement impact, exemplifying the type of payer network practice litigation and policy disputes central to the topic.
items.summary: Corewell Health and UnitedHealthcare resolved a contract dispute that had resulted in Corewell hospitals being placed out-of-network for patients for several months. The dispute centered on reimbursement rates and contract terms between the Michigan-based health system and the major national payer. The resolution restores in-network status for Corewell facilities under UnitedHealthcare plans, affecting patients' access and out-of-pocket costs. This case exemplifies payer practices that penalize facilities through network exclusions during contract negotiations—a key revenue-cycle issue for hospital systems.
